response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and distributed systems, but the role demands deep, hands-on C++ expertise as its core requirement. His C++ exposure is minimal—limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project—and C++ does not appear in his listed skills. The position additionally requires industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, and familiarity with industrial protocols such as MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, and OPC UA, none of which are evidenced in his resume. While he demonstrates strong software craftsmanship principles, agile practices, and event-driven architecture experience, these transferable strengths are insufficient to offset the fundamental mismatch in core language and domain expertise.
strengths: Proven software craftsmanship background including TDD, CI/CD, and agile practices across high-scale distributed systems at Stripe and Shopify
Event-driven and high-performance systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Full-stack versatility with REST API design, Node.js, React, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK), partially aligning with the role's advantageous skills
concerns: C++ is absent from his listed skills and documented experience with it is limited to a 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project—far below the seniority and depth required
No evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 compliance, or industrial communication protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
Career focus is exclusively on web and SaaS product engineering with no apparent exposure to embedded systems, on-premises industrial platforms, or cybersecurity standards

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 experience
Strong distributed systems and event-driven architecture background
